title: mumei review pipeline output
description: Phase 5 / /mumei:examine pipeline verdict, persisted at .mumei/specs/<feature>/reviews/<ts>.json (spec vehicle) or .mumei/plans/<slug>/reviews/<ts>.json (plan vehicle).

wave.description: Wave under review, or 'all' for end-of-feature pipelines.

iteration.description: Review iter index. Current orchestrator caps at 3 (REQ-7.6 short-circuit) but historical reviews under archive may carry higher values from earlier iter caps.

iter_head.description: git rev-parse HEAD at iter completion. Used by Stage 0 detector skip logic in iter N+1.

diff_hash.description: Content hash of the review surface (git tree id of the working-tree snapshot) this review was produced against, from mumei_review_diff_hash. push-guard requires each always-on reviewer's cost-log after-record to carry a matching diff_hash and the repo state at push time to hash to the same value. Normally a sha256 hex; on hosts lacking shasum/sha256sum a cksum fallback is used, so the exact format is not constrained. Optional: omitted when git/HEAD is unavailable; older reviews predate the field.

memory_candidates_count.description: Total memory_candidates emitted across all reviewers this iter, stamped at Stage 6.5. push-guard's curator-completeness advisory uses it to tell 'no candidates' from 'candidates emitted but curation skipped'. Optional: omitted by reviews that ran no curation stage.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of the review verdict. Older archived reviews may omit this field.

reviewers.description: Per-reviewer verdict map. Keys are reviewer short names (spec-compliance / security / adversarial).

trace.description: Falsifiable basis for a HIGH/CRITICAL finding (pillar C, REQ-22.1): the input -> bad-output / source -> sink path the issue-validator checks on its REPRODUCIBLE axis. Distinct from `message`/`evidence`.

source.description: Detector ground-truth marker: 'semgrep' / 'osv-scanner' / 'structural-integrity' for deterministic findings.

trace.description: Falsifiable basis for a HIGH/CRITICAL finding (pillar C, REQ-22.1): the input -> bad-output / source -> sink path the issue-validator checks on its REPRODUCIBLE axis. Distinct from `message`/`evidence`.

source.description: Detector ground-truth marker: 'semgrep' / 'osv-scanner' / 'structural-integrity' for deterministic findings.

next_iter_reviewers.description: Reviewer set to launch in iter N+1. Always contains 'adversarial' (REQ-7.3 invariant).

detector_skipped.description: REQ-7.5: true when iter 2+ skipped Stage 0 because no detector-relevant file changed since iter N-1.

detector_reused_from.description: Path to the previous iter's detector report when detector_skipped == true.

detector_report.description: Path to <ts>-detectors.json with raw semgrep / osv-scanner findings.

short_circuited_from.description: Path to the prior review JSON when this entry is a REQ-7.7 short-circuit synthetic record.

confidence_ceiling.description: One-line honesty disclaimer (pillar C, REQ-22.10): names the Claude-family shared blind spot and the real-bug detection ceiling. Never claims human review is unnecessary.

residual.description: Residual exposition (pillar D, REQ-23): signals objective verification cannot guarantee, for human review. Optional: archived reviews and synthetic short-circuit records (iter-1-all-PASS) omit it; for a full (non-short-circuit) review the orchestrator emits it non-empty via the always-on ai-blindspot-ceiling. Consumers must null-check.

source.description: Originating signal: advisory / validator-unsure / validator-skip / reviewer-filtered / ceiling.

ref.description: Originating finding id, reviewer name, or "-" for the ceiling item.

note.description: Verbatim original reason / message for human spot-check.
